title: Gluteal Tendinopathy and Lateral Hip Pain
synonyms: Gluteal Tendinopathy and Lateral Hip Pain

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Lateral hip pain is commonly related to gluteal tendinopathy and compression-sensitive lateral hip structures. It often responds to load and compression modification with progressive strengthening.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Lateral hip pain with walking, stairs, side-lying, or standing on one leg
- Tenderness over the greater trochanter
- Reduced tolerance to hills, step-ups, or prolonged standing
- Possible night pain when lying on the affected side

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Consider lumbar referral, fracture, septic joint, inflammatory disease, or major weakness if the pattern is atypical
- Compression such as side-lying and hip adduction can be major aggravators
- Pain relief from injection does not replace load-based rehabilitation

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Screen trauma, weight-bearing safety, and major red flags
- Advise reducing compressive positions and temporarily modifying aggravating load
- Escalate if fracture, severe weakness, or systemic features are suspected

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Assess single-leg tolerance, hip abductor function, gait, and irritability
- Start graded hip abductor strengthening and compression-load management
- Refer for imaging or medical review when pain is severe, diagnosis uncertain, or progress poor

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Lateral hip pain often improves when the patient stops repeatedly compressing the tendon between sessions
